item.title: Stocks skid to four-month low as oil shock spooks investors
item.description: Investors are shifting out of bonds and other assets exposed to inflation as global markets brace for prolonged disruption to energy supplies while war rages in the Middle East.

item.title: Exclusive: Japan considers cutting inflation-linked bond buybacks as demand rises, sources say
item.description: Japan is considering a reduction in buybacks of inflation-linked government bonds as investor demand swells amid rising inflation expectations, two sources familiar вҖӢwith the matter said on Monday.

item.title: Dollar poised for rally as escalating Middle East conflict spurs haven demand
item.description: The dollar was poised for a rebound on Monday as retaliatory threats escalated in the Middle East crisis, damping risk sentiment and boosting demand for haven assets.

item.title: Asia shares skid, yields rise as Gulf war escalates
item.description: Share markets slid in Asia on Monday while U.S. bond yields hit eight-month peaks as the United вҖӢStates and Iran traded escalating threats and Israel planned for weeks more fighting, sending oil prices on another roller-coaster ride.

item.title: Activist Elliott takes multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys, WSJ reports
item.description: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has a multibillion-dollar investment вҖӢin Synopsys , the Wall Street Journal reported on вҖҢSunday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oil prices rise after US, Iran threaten to hit energy targets in the Middle East
item.description: Oil prices rose вҖӢon Monday after вҖҢU.S. President Donald Trump and Iran threatened вҖӢto attack вҖӢenergy facilities in the вҒ Middle East, вҖӢescalating the war.

item.title: New Zealand struggles to regain economic mojo without housing recovery
item.description: For decades, New Zealand has relied on inflating the housing market to engineer a recovery during downturns, but the playbook has failed this time, putting policymakers in a quandary just as the Middle East war adds a new layer of uncertainty.

item.title: Musk says SpaceX and Tesla to build advanced chip factories in Austin
item.description: The comments followed MuskвҖҷs announcement a day earlier вҖӢof plans to build 'Terafab,' an advanced AI chip complex in Austin.

item.title: US SEC concludes four-year-old probe into EV startup Faraday Future with no action
item.description: Electric vehicle startup Faraday Future said on Sunday вҖӢthe U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission вҖҢhas concluded its investigation into the company with no action against it.

item.title: NZ's Fonterra ups annual earnings outlook, flags potential Middle East disruptions
item.description: New Zealand's Fonterra Co-operative Group raised its вҖҢfull-year earnings outlook on Monday after posting a steady rise in half-year profit, while flagging potential impacts from the Middle East conflict on supply chains and costs.

item.title: Oil prices to rise further on Monday as Mideast war escalates
item.description: Oil prices look set to rise further on Monday, having closed before the вҖҢweekend at their highest in nearly four years, after U.S. and Iranian threats to target energy facilities, analysts said on Sunday.

item.title: Tencent integrates WeChat with OpenClaw AI agent amid China tech battle
item.description: Tencent launched a tool on Sunday to integrate its WeChat messaging platform with the OpenClaw вҖӢagent, deepening its push into AI agents вҖҢthat have become a key battleground among China's technology companies.

item.title: China pledges more balanced trade and further opening of the economy after record surplus
item.description: Chinese Premier Li Qiang pledged on Sunday to further open вҖӢthe country's economy to foreign firms and pursue more balanced trade with its global partners, after a year marked вҖҢby trade friction and tariff wars with the United States and European Union in particular.

item.title: Volkswagen CEO tells newspaper German carmakers should look to Chinese planning
item.description: Germany's car industry could learn from China's disciplined industrial вҖӢplanning, Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume вҖҢsaid in an interview released on Sunday, as the automaker pushes ahead with вҖӢdeep restructuring to stay вҖӢcompetitive.

item.title: Fed Chair Powell lauds Volcker's 'willingness to resist'
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Saturday lauded former Fed Chair Paul Volcker's "willingness to resist" political pressure as he fought inflation in the вҖӢ1980s, remarks that have particular resonance as Powell presses his own defense вҖҢof the Fed's independence amid the Trump administration's efforts to get him to lower rates.

item.title: OpenAI to expand ads on ChatGPT to all free and low-cost users, The Information reports
item.description: OpenAI has told вҖӢadvertising agencies it вҖҢis increasing the number вҖӢof users вҖӢshown ads, with вҒ ChatGPT set вҖӢto display вҖӢads to all users on its вҖӢfree and вҖӢlow-cost versions in вҖҢthe вҒ coming weeks, The Information reported on Saturday, вҖӢciting вҖӢpeople вҒ briefed on the вҖӢmatter.

item.title: Elon Musk offers to pay TSA salaries amid budget battle, airport lineups
item.description: Billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk, the worldвҖҷs richest person, said on Saturday he would cover the paychecks of U.S. Transportation Security Administration вҖӢofficers during their second unpaid work stoppage in six months вҖҢamid a protracted federal funding lapse.

item.title: OpenAI to nearly double workforce to 8,000 by end-2026, FT reports
item.description: Artificial intelligence start-up вҖӢOpenAI plans вҖҢto nearly double вҖӢits вҖӢworkforce to 8,000 from вҒ 4,500 вҖӢby the вҖӢend of 2026, the Financial вҖӢTimes вҖӢreported on Saturday, вҖҢciting вҒ two people with knowledge of вҖӢthe вҖӢmatter.

item.title: Iran war's energy impact forces world to pay up, cut consumption
item.description: The war in the Middle East has triggered a nightmare scenario for the global energy system, slashing so much supply that consumers around the world must both pay up big and lower consumption.

item.title: Mexico's Femsa cuts workers at Spin fintech division
item.description: Mexican retailer and bottler Femsa said on Friday it is laying вҖӢoff workers in its fintech unit Spin, which launched вҖҢa digital wallet app for payments and financial transactions in 2021.

item.title: United Airlines to cut 5% of scheduled flights as fuel prices soar
item.description: United Airlines CEO Scott вҖӢKirby said on Friday the airline вҖҢwill cancel about 5% of planned flights in the short term, as jet fuel prices surge due to вҖӢthe Middle East conflict.

item.title: Exclusive: Pentagon to adopt Palantir AIВ as core US military system, memo says
item.description: PalantirвҖҷs Maven artificial intelligence system will become an official program of record, Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve вҖӢFeinberg said in a letter to Pentagon leaders, a move that locks in long-term use of PalantirвҖҷs weapons-targeting technology across вҖҢthe U.S. military.

item.title: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund posts first monthly loss in over three years
item.description: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund posted its first monthly loss in more than three years in February, the fund's website showed on Friday, amid surging investor вҖӢworries over the sector's liquidity strains.

item.title: Musk found liable to Twitter shareholders in fraud lawsuit over $44 billion takeover, Bloomberg says
item.description: A federal jury found Elon Musk liable on claims he defrauded Twitter shareholders by trying to drive down the social media вҖӢcompany's stock price so he could renegotiate or back out of a $44 billion takeover in вҖҢ2022, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Jury says Musk misled Twitter investors before 2022 buyout, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: A jury has concluded Elon Musk вҖӢdefrauded Twitter investors when вҖҢhe disparaged the company in 2022 in вҖӢan effort to вҖӢbuy the social media platform вҒ for a lower вҖӢprice than his original $44 вҖӢbillion bid, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Sezzle dismisses auditor Baker Tilly, appoints PwC for 2026
item.description: Sezzle has dismissed its independent auditor Baker Tilly and вҖӢinstead appointed PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) for 2026, вҖҢthe buy now, pay later company said in a filing on вҖӢMonday.

item.title: Nuclear reactor developer XвҖ‘energy files for US IPO
item.description: X-energy вҖӢhas filed for вҖҢan initial public offering in вҖӢthe United States, вҖӢit said on вҒ Friday, as the вҖӢreactor developer aims вҖӢto tap growing investor enthusiasm for nuclear вҖӢpower.

item.title: EyePoint sues rival Ocular Therapeutix, alleging false claims about eye drug
item.description: EyePoint filed a lawsuit on Friday accusing rival Ocular Therapeutix of spreading вҖӢfalse or misleading information about EyePoint and its вҖҢlead experimental eye drug.

item.title: Super Micro co-founder Liaw resigns from board after US charge for smuggling AI chips to China
item.description: Super Micro вҖӢComputer said on Friday that вҖҢYih-Shyan Liaw has resigned from its board, effective immediately, after вҖӢthe co-founder was charged вҖӢwith helping smuggle billions of вҒ dollars of AI chips вҖӢto China.

item.title: US appeals court tosses FTC order against Intuit over TurboTax advertising
item.description: A U.S. appeals court вҖӢon Friday вҖҢthrew out a Federal Trade вҖӢCommission order вҖӢbarring Intuit from вҒ deceptively advertising вҖӢits popular TurboTax вҖӢproducts as "free" for simple tax returns.
